steps.title: Identify the slope from line k's equation
steps.content: Line $k$ is defined by $y = \frac{1}{4}x + 1$. This equation is in slope-intercept form $y = mx + b$, where $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the y-intercept. By direct comparison, the slope of line $k$ is $\frac{1}{4}$.

steps.title: Apply the parallel lines property
steps.content: Parallel lines have equal slopes. Since line $j$ is parallel to line $k$, line $j$ must have the same slope as line $k$. Therefore, the slope of line $j$ is $\frac{1}{4}$, which equals 0.25.
